mechanism_narrative: An increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand and rising corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in RSAFS coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in at least 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm a contraction in both labor and supply conditions, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining PAYEMS and INDPRO does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of joint-down months, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05) in a two-factor logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: During elevated inflation, an increase in the unemployment rate signals easing wage pressures that reduce the probability of further Federal Reserve tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 200, instances of rising UNRATE are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly level of new housing starts (HOUST) below 2000 thousand units signals weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST < 2000.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and revenue growth for industrial firms, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates above 4.5% and declining retail sales (RSAFS) jointly compresses corporate profit margins and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and declining RSAFS fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations during any 2-year window.
